title: Cloning to silence ownership uncertainty
bad_pattern: Add `.clone()` or wrap in `Arc<Mutex<_>>` before proving the ownership boundary.
good_pattern: Model the owner, borrower, and mutation point first. Prefer borrowing, narrow scopes, or owned transfer before shared mutable state.
rule: Clones and shared ownership need a reason tied to data flow, not compiler appeasement.

title: Stringly typed state machines
bad_pattern: Represent internal modes as arbitrary `String` values and branch on string comparisons.
good_pattern: Use an enum or newtype with explicit variants, conversion boundaries, and exhaustive matches.
rule: Make invalid states unrepresentable when the set of states is known.

title: Assuming HashMap iteration order
bad_pattern: Expose `HashMap` iteration order in user-visible output or tests.
good_pattern: Use `BTreeMap`, sort entries before rendering, or document that order is intentionally unspecified.
rule: User-visible output and tests must not rely on unspecified collection ordering.

title: Unwraps in MCP handlers
bad_pattern: Use `unwrap`, `expect`, or `panic` while parsing tool input or building output.
good_pattern: Return `IlluError::Invalid` for bad inputs or a user-facing tool message for expected misses.
rule: Tool handlers are user-facing boundaries and must preserve error context.

title: Performance claims without measurement
bad_pattern: Claim an implementation is faster because it looks simpler or avoids an allocation.
good_pattern: Attach benchmark output, complexity reasoning, profiling data, or a before/after measurement.
rule: Performance claims require evidence and must name the workload being improved.
